en.extract:
The Baliem whistler or Balim whistler (Pachycephala balim) is a species of bird of the whistler family Pachycephalidae that is endemic to New Guinea.


Taxonomy and systematics
The Baliem whistler was formerly considered as a subspecies of the Australian golden whistler and also of the mangrove golden and the yellow-throated whistler. It has since been re-classified as a separate species by the IOC in 2016. Many other authorities do not yet recognize this split and re-classification.


Distribution
The Baliem whistler is found in the Snow Mountains of Papua Province in Indonesia.

The '''Baliem whistler''' or '''Balim whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala balim''''') is a species of [[bird]] of the whistler family [[Pachycephalidae]] that is [[endemic]] to [[New Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.4|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.4|doi-access=free}}</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The Baliem whistler was formerly considered as a subspecies of the [[Australian golden whistler]] and also of the [[mangrove golden whistler|mangrove golden]] and the [[yellow-throated whistler]]. It has since been re-classified as a separate species by the IOC in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/species-updates/|title=Species Updates « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|access-date=2016-11-08}}</ref> Many other authorities do not yet recognize this split and re-classification.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://avibase.bsc-eoc.org/species.jsp?avibaseid=2A5D6D4A879A7785|title=Pachycephala pectoralis balim - Avibase|website=avibase.bsc-eoc.org|access-date=2016-11-08}}</ref>

==Distribution==
The Baliem whistler is found in the [[Snow Mountains]] of [[Papua Province]] in Indonesia.
